summary: Configure-only PR that changed several objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, removed one NonMatching object entry, and produced a bot report showing linked-code and linked-data increases. The PR was closed without a recorded merge.

key_files.role: Updated MatchingFor object list entries, changing selected source files from NonMatching to Matching and deleting one NonMatching ft_3C61 entry.

what_changed.text: Removed Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_3C61.c") from the MatchingFor object list.
A decomp-dev bot report for GALE01 reported linked code 43.26% (+0.90%, +35120 bytes), linked data 35.06% (+0.07%, +824 bytes), and the text 'No changes'.

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to configure.py link-status changes rather than mixing in source decomp edits.
Grouped several apparently already-linkable or 'free' object status flips into a single small build-configuration change.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A link-status cleanup PR can be configure-only when the work is to promote already-suitable objects from NonMatching to Matching; the evidence should name each object and rely on the generated link report rather than implying new source-level decomp changes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use configure.py Object status flips from NonMatching to Matching to link source files once they are ready, keeping the change localized to MatchingFor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR slice does not include objdiff/build logs for individual files, only the bot linked-code/data report.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed linked code 43.26% (+0.90%, +35120 bytes), linked data 35.06% (+0.07%, +824 bytes), and 'No changes'.

review_feedback.text: Handling this in a separate PR.

evidence_refs: metadata_json:pr state CLOSED merged_at null changed_files=1
human_pr_text:Handling this in a separate PR
notes: The diff and changed-file metadata are strong for what changed in configure.py.
There are no review comments and no human explanation for why each object was safe to link.
The bot report gives aggregate linked-code/data deltas but does not provide per-object objdiff or build evidence.
The PR was closed with merged_at null in the loaded metadata, so this intake does not claim the PR was merged or accepted.
The human_pr_text excerpt labels the comment author PsiLupan, while PR metadata identifies the PR author as BR-; this record uses BR- as PR author and PsiLupan as issue-comment author.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Configure-only linking PRs should identify exact Object status flips
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2589 shows a narrow configure.py-only link-status update: several Object entries were changed from NonMatching to Matching, one NonMatching ft_3C61 entry was removed, and the bot report recorded aggregate linked-code/data increases. Curators can use this as a PR-specific example of keeping link-status cleanup separate from source edits.

source_update_candidates.title: configure.py MatchingFor owns Matching/NonMatching object link-status entries
source_update_candidates.text: PR evidence shows configure.py MatchingFor containing Object(Matching, path) and Object(NonMatching, path) entries, with a configure-only PR changing object link status for several source paths.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path fact for future PR intake and curation, but should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the listed source files were newly decompiled in this PR; the slice only shows configure.py status changes.
Do not promote per-file match acceptance or validation beyond the aggregate bot report because the slice lacks per-object objdiff/build logs.
Do not infer reviewer intent from the 'Handling this in a separate PR' comment beyond the literal statement that some handling was deferred.
